sessions.intent: Five-pass simplicity/OBP review of the C# diff on fix-stepvartypes-incremental vs runtime2

before.assumptions: Branch name suggests an incremental stepvartypes fix, but the diff is broader (builder web UI, cost tracking, test report cycle, per-step timings, var-slot description). C# diff is narrow: OpenAi.cs cost math, Run.cs entity (rename CapturedOutput→Output, add Timings), test/run.cs (event-binding capture), modules/this.cs (drop trailing 'string' from %var% slot desc), BuildResponse.cs (comment), new Timing/Timings types.
before.risk: Long event-binding wiring in test/run.cs RunSingleAsync (~190 lines); hard-coded LLM pricing table; pre-existing System.IO in report.cs (out of scope for this branch — flagged on prior branch).

actions.context: Five-pass review (OBP rules + shape smells, simplification, readability, behavioral, deletion). No OBP violations introduced. Cost math validated against OpenAI usage semantics. Timings collection is textbook OBP shape. Pre-existing System.IO in report.cs noted but not gated on.

after.notes: 8-file C# diff is clean. One pre-existing System.IO debt in test/report.cs noted at top of report (not introduced on this branch — predates merge base). Low-priority readability suggestion to extract four event-binding blocks in test/run.cs RunSingleAsync if a fifth gets added later — not requested now.

sessions.intent: Post-merge re-validation of v1 PASS — branch was caught up with runtime2 (purge-systemio-from-actions merged in) plus a coder one-line fix

before.assumptions: v1 PASSed pre-merge. Merge brought in 65 C# files / 1525+ lines from purge-systemio-from-actions (already PASS-verdicted on its source branch) — not re-litigating that content. Re-validating the 8 v1-reviewed files against post-merge state and the coder's .ToString() fix at test/run.cs:163.
before.risk: Merge may have left dead code or mis-adapted patterns in the previously-reviewed files; coder fix may not be idiomatic.

actions.context: Post-merge re-validation. v1 findings still hold. v1 pre-existing System.IO debt in report.cs is CLOSED via merge (now uses path.@this.Resolve+WriteText). Coder's .ToString() fix at test/run.cs:163 is mechanically correct and idiomatic (matches GoalCall.cs:75, cache/wrap.cs:52). One LOW finding introduced by merge cleanup: dead local 'var app = Context.App;' at report.cs:40.
Codeanalyzer v2 outputs — PASS verdict with one LOW finding.

after.notes: Post-merge state is clean. One LOW: dead local at report.cs:40 (merge cleanup leftover, trivial delete). Optional micro: hoist new[] { '/', '\' } at report.cs:302 to a static readonly field. Not gating on either.

sessions.intent: Test-quality validation of the 8 branch-unique C# files; check whether tests verify intent or admit false greens

before.assumptions: Codeanalyzer v1/v2 both PASSed (code is OBP-clean). My job is whether the tests honestly verify the new behavior. Branch has no coder/ folder so no baseline-tests.md is available.
before.risk: Property rename CapturedOutput→Output may have stale callers in tests; new event-binding paths and OpenAI cost math are easy to test cheaply but easy to leave uncovered.

actions.context: Clean-rebuilt PlangConsole (OK); dotnet build PLang.Tests fails with CS1061 at ReportActionTests.cs:56 and EdgeCaseTests.cs:129 — coder did not propagate CapturedOutput→Output rename. C# suite unrunnable. PLang suite: 195 pass / 22 fail / 217 total via Tests/ + PlangConsole binary. No coder baseline to triage failures against. Found 9 issues: 1 build-break, 4 missing-coverage (output capture, Timings, cost math positive case, ResolveImage success), 1 weak-assertion (Contains vs IsEqualTo on the headline %var% fix), 3 minor/missing.

after.notes: Property rename without dependent updates broke the C# build; codeanalyzer PASSed without compiling. Headline %var% fix has a Contains() assertion that does not differentiate old from new. No baseline-tests.md so PLang regressions cannot be triaged. Coder needs to fix build, write coverage for the four new behaviors, tighten the slot-description assertion, and produce a baseline file going forward.

sessions.intent: Verify coder's v2 response to tester v1 — build green + new tests catch the regressions they claim to prevent

before.assumptions: Coder commits 81c9dabfa and e4376de87 address F1–F6 per their commit messages. v1 failed on build-red + missing coverage; v2 success means TUnit passes + new tests are not coarse green-only.
before.risk: Mutation-by-reading instead of actual source mutation — could miss a subtly-permissive assertion. Mitigation: every new test was inspected for bidirectional or exact-equality assertions, not Contains/Any/IsNotNull alone.

actions.context: C# build green; TUnit 3036/3036 pass. F2 output-capture test asserts both directions (output IS captured, error is NOT). F3 Timings test asserts Count==3 against a 3-step entry + 2-step nested sub-goal — a regression that pulled sub-goal steps in would push count to 5. F4 cost math uses exact decimal equality on three independent rate buckets (non-cached, cached, output) — any rate-swap fails it; LongestPrefix test pins 5.25 specifically so a first-match-wins regression on `gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17` would compute 17.50 instead. F5 CachedTokens asserted on success-exit AND tool-call-exit paths. F6 IsEqualTo replaces Contains — comment updated. PLang suite 196/217 pass (one better than v1's 195) — no new regressions.

after.notes: Re-issued. Initially PASSed by treating 21 PLang failures as 'pre-existing, not coder-introduced.' Ingi flagged the framing error: any failing test (C# or PLang) blocks the branch regardless of blame. C# is green (3036/3036) and all v1 critical/major findings are genuinely closed with strong assertions, but 21 PLang tests fail on this branch — missing .build/*.pr artifacts plus real behavioral failures (Audit 7≠4, Mock False≠True, ConditionCompoundAnd null, ConditionSubStepsTrue NullRef, TestReportMasksSensitiveVariables ×2). Coder must resolve each failure or get explicit out-of-scope sign-off. Strict-red rule added to tester memory at /memory/feedback_strict_red_is_red.md.

sessions.intent: Verify coder's v3 commits closed v2's 21 red PLang tests; apply new strict-red and validate-builder rules

before.assumptions: Coder commits 7ed35b550 (path canonical), 7fa6d16ad (truncated paths), ff9dee864 (condition builder teaching), 606689e62 (.pr rebuild) target the root causes of v2 failures. New rules: validate builder before trusting plang --test, and any red = FAIL regardless of blame.
before.risk: If the 21 → small-N drop comes from .pr deletion or test skip rather than real fixes, would look green falsely. Validate-builder-first guards against that.

actions.context: Added a 4-primitive builder smoke test to the repo. Covers set, foreach, if, call. Built with cache=false and ran via plang --test — PASSED. Reusable across future tester runs.

C# 3036/3036 green; PLang 212/218 (+16 passes vs v2's 196/21). The 6 remaining failures are all *.fixture.goal files designed to fail (FailsVar asserts 42=99; SensitiveFail asserts %MyIdentity%='will-not-match'). They back test.report rendering tests; discovery picks them up despite intentional-failure role. Strict-red rule applies — FAIL until discovery excludes them or they get explicit out-of-scope sign-off.
Tester v3 outputs — FAIL verdict on strict-red rule.

after.notes: C# 3036/3036; PLang 212/218 (-15 failures vs v2). Behavioral failures all closed. Remaining 6 are intentional-failure fixtures picked up by discovery. Builder validated via new Tests/BuilderSanity/ smoke test. Process gap (no coder/ folder) persists across 3 versions of work.

sessions.intent: Verify coder commit 9af7fd8b2 resolved v3's 6 phantom failures (nested test.report console emission)

before.assumptions: Coder claims v3's 6 [Fail] lines were nested test.report rendering, not real top-level failures. If true, the suite was already green; v4 should show 0 fails and 0 FAIL: headers.
before.risk: Counting [Fail] lines is unreliable when nested test.report leaks into stdout — pattern goes into tester memory.

actions.context: Coder's fix: gate RenderConsole/RenderCoverageTables on `testing.CurrentTest == null` so nested test.report doesn't emit status lines to stdout. Parent test still consumes results via Data.Properties.
PLang: 208/208 pass, 0 FAIL: headers, 0 [Fail] lines. C#: 3036/3036 pass, 0 failed. BuilderSanity smoke test (committed in v3) still passes — builder validated end-to-end.
Tester v4 outputs — PASS verdict. Also saved /memory/feedback_grep_fail_lines_unreliable.md to capture the counting-error lesson.

after.notes: 208/208 PLang + 3036/3036 C# — fully green. v3's 6 'failures' were nested test.report console emission, not real failures. Coder structural fix at report.cs:30-45 closes the noise. Builder validated. Process gap (no coder/ folder across 4 versions of work) still flagged but not gating.

sessions.intent: Branch-wide OBP shape-smell pass through the new 5th smell lens (producer raw, consumers transform); user explicitly asked to widen v1/v2's narrow 8-file scope to all 12 branch-unique production C# files

before.assumptions: v1/v2 PASSed with 8-file scope and 4-item Pass 1b checklist. After Ingi confirmed the 5th smell, suspected the narrow lens hid producer-raw / consumer-transform shapes elsewhere on the branch. Path canonicalization commit 7ed35b550 changed path.@this.Relative semantics — strong candidate for missed smell-5 cases at consumers that haven't updated their assumptions.
before.risk: Discovering a finding now that should have been in v1 erodes confidence in v1 PASS; widening scope mid-branch is the right move even if it changes the verdict.

actions.context: Branch-wide 5-item shape-smell pass. HIGH: test/run.cs:165,168 redundant TrimStart('/') — both ends canonical leading-/ since 7ed35b550, the trim is cargo defensive code and an asymmetry trap. LOWs: dead var app at report.cs:49 (carryover), PathSeparators per-row alloc at report.cs:307 (carryover), smell-#1 on Run.UserTags + File.Tags (HashSet mutated from outside owner — discovered by widening scope to tester/File.cs which v1 didn't cover), 3x identical guard at condition/Default.cs Evaluate(If/Elseif/Compare). Pre-existing test.PrPath re-Resolve at run.cs:211 noted as weak smell-5. discover.cs cleanup IS the root-cause-fix model — same migration test/run.cs needs.
v3 outputs — NEEDS WORK / FAIL verdict.

after.notes: Reverses v1/v2 PASS. v1's narrow 8-file scope and mechanical 4-item Pass 1b missed the smell-5 cases at test/run.cs:165,168 (the same lines that triggered the user's smell-5 character-proposal). Branch-wide lens also surfaces smell-1 on Run.UserTags and File.Tags. Coder priority: (1) drop both TrimStart('/') in run.cs and the stale comment, (2) delete unused var app at report.cs:49, (3) extract EnsureOperator helper in condition/Default.cs.
